operating_state: Net sales increased 7.3% to $116.4 billion driven by AWS growth and advertising services expansion, while online stores declined. North America segment posted operating loss versus prior year income, while International segment declined with operating loss. Operating income compressed to $3.7 billion from $8.9 billion as fulfillment network inefficiencies and supply chain constraints offset revenue gains.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased to $112.8 billion from $99.7 billion, with fulfillment costs rising due to wage inflation, transportation increases, and network inefficiencies from constrained labor markets. Technology and content expenses increased driven by infrastructure investments, while stock-based compensation rose. Extended server depreciation schedules from four to five years and networking equipment from five to six years reduced depreciation expense by $973 million.
material_events: Completed MGM Holdings acquisition for $6.1 billion cash in March 2022, acquiring video content and goodwill. Board authorized new $10 billion share repurchase program replacing previous authorization, with $2.7 billion repurchased during the quarter. Board approved 20-for-1 stock split effective June 2022 subject to shareholder approval at May Annual Meeting.
macro_exposures: Rivian equity investment generated $7.6 billion valuation loss contributing to $8.6 billion total other expense. Foreign exchange headwinds reduced net sales by $1.8 billion with particular impact on International segment performance. Operations face continued pressure from inflation, global supply chain constraints, and labor market tightness driving wage increases and fulfillment network inefficiencies.
forward_outlook: Q2 2022 net sales guidance of $116.0-$121.0 billion represents 3-7% growth with foreign exchange headwind anticipated, while operating income guidance ranges from negative $1.0 billion to positive $3.0 billion. Expanded commercial paper program capacity and unsecured revolving credit facility. AWS performance obligations totaled $88.9 billion with weighted-average remaining contract life of 3.8 years.
uncertainty_profile: Tax contingencies of $3.3 billion remain subject to examination by multiple jurisdictions including IRS, with European Commission state aid appeal pending. Multiple patent infringement cases and antitrust litigation ongoing with potential for material damages. Italian Competition Authority fine subject to appeal process.
